items.title: npm run dev:all starts the app on native Windows
items.precondition: Native Windows (PowerShell/cmd), Node 20+, Docker Desktop running, deps installed, .env present.
steps: 1. Open PowerShell in the repo root.
3. Watch the output through the Postgres + migration steps.
items.expected: No 'sh' error and no npm ENOENT; the launcher prints the 'DevEasy is starting' banner and the app is reachable at http://localhost:1234.
items.waiver: Deferred — not verifiable from the macOS dev environment. Code implemented (sleepSync + shell-aware spawn); typecheck clean. Pending a Windows hardware run.

items.title: Claude session spawns and streams on Windows
items.precondition: App running (T1); Claude CLI on PATH (claude.cmd) and logged in; at least one project tracked.
steps: 1. Open the app, pick a project, start a session.
3. Observe the streamed reply; let a session auto-name occur.
items.expected: claude.cmd is found and spawned (no ENOENT); the reply streams; session auto-naming (runHeadless) works.
items.waiver: Deferred — not verifiable from the macOS dev environment. PATHEXT resolver unit-tested; spawn sites wired. Pending a Windows hardware run.

items.title: Config injected via junction + hardlink with no admin, propagates live, idempotent
items.precondition: App running on a NON-admin Windows account, Developer Mode OFF. PROJECTS_ROOT on the same drive as the repo. A project tracked.
steps: 2. In the project folder, run `dir` / check: `.claude\skills` and `.claude\agents` are junctions (`<JUNCTION>` in `dir`), `CLAUDE.md` exists.
3. Confirm `fsutil hardlink list <project>\CLAUDE.md` lists the shared repo CLAUDE.md.
4. Edit the shared CLAUDE.md in the Agent Manager and save.
6. Close and re-open the project in DevEasy.
items.expected: Injection succeeds without elevation; folders are junctions and CLAUDE.md is a hardlink; the Agent Manager edit appears in the project's CLAUDE.md; re-opening throws no CONFIG_INJECTION_CONFLICT.
items.notes: Core sharing mechanism — the highest-value check. Verify this one first on Windows.
items.waiver: Deferred — not verifiable from the macOS dev environment. junction/hardlink logic + inode/content idempotency implemented; POSIX path covered by the existing suite. Pending a Windows hardware run.

items.title: Terminal opens in the project dir and Ctrl+C reaps children
steps: 1. Open a terminal tab for a project.
2. Confirm the shell starts in the project directory (run `cd` / `pwd`).
3. Start a session and/or terminal, then press Ctrl+C in the server console where dev:all runs.
4. Check Task Manager / `tasklist` for leftover claude or shell child processes.
items.expected: Terminal starts in the project folder; Ctrl+C (and Ctrl+Break) stop the server and leave no orphaned claude/PTY processes.
items.notes: Console-window close (X) is a known, documented exception.
items.waiver: Deferred — not verifiable from the macOS dev environment. ComSpec/PowerShell fallback, cwd validation, and SIGBREAK reaping implemented; terminal unit suite green. Pending a Windows hardware run.

items.title: macOS/Linux behavior unchanged (regression)
steps: 1. Open a project; confirm `.claude/skills`, `.claude/agents`, and `CLAUDE.md` are symlinks (`ls -l`).
2. Start a session and open a terminal.
items.expected: Injection still uses POSIX symlinks; session and terminal start exactly as before.
items.evidence: Unit suite 70/70 green; typecheck clean. Live app not exercised this run.
items.waiver: Deferred — POSIX branch left byte-for-byte unchanged and covered by the existing unit suite (70/70), but the running app was not exercised in this session. Low risk.
